issues.title: Define echo/reply packet type for delay measurement
issues.author: ihlar
issues.body:
A third POEM packet type for endpoint-to-endpoint delay measurement. One endpoint sends an echo, the peer replies. On-path observers time the echo and reply to measure RTT and split it into upstream/downstream components.

Payload bits could be used to encode reply delay, possibly using some logarithmic encoding. This would make measurements more precise than the spin bit. 
Report packets can be added for delay as well. 
Sending frequency could be left to endpoints or be defined by the protocol. It could range from once per RTT to occasional pings.

issues.title: Guidance on reordering
issues.author: ihlar
issues.body:
Packet reordering around marker packets causes the observer to count intervals of different sizes (e.g., 63 and 65 instead of 64 and 64). This doesn't represent actual loss but inflates the upstream loss estimate in one interval and deflates it in the next.
Simple averaging over multiple intervals is probably sufficient to handle this, but the document could provide explicit guidance for observers.

issues.title: Alternative report design
issues.author: ihlar
issues.body:
Instead of dedicated report packets, the observer could rewrite the loss count field in marker packets and flip the MSB of the version to signal modification. The rewritten value represents downstream loss (total - upstream) as computed by the observer.
Receivers get more frequent and detailed loss information from the network, but needs to compute ratios themselves.
In a chain of observers, the downstream observers will lose total-loss information.
